exemplar.title: How can mathematical modelling be used to investigate the electrocardiogram (ECG) complex of patients with hypertension?

detailedExemplarData.title: How can mathematical modelling be used to investigate the electrocardiogram (ECG) complex of patients with hypertension?

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear narrative with logical progression from rationale to modelling and evaluation
Well-labelled figures and a comprehensive contents page aid navigation
Aim clearly stated and effectively motivates the mathematical approach
weaknesses: Redundant âConclusionâ entries and uneven page numbering disrupt organization
Extended medical background and personal anecdotes detract from conciseness
Occasional digressions into non-mathematical detail lengthen the report unnecessarily
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the exploration is coherent and organized (band 3), significant conciseness issues (band 2) and organizational lapses (duplicate headings, tangential content) lower the overall presentation to a âsome basic organizationâ level. The narrative flow is good, but the excessive non-mathematical material and minor structural duplications justify a final score of 2.

strengths: Mostly consistent use of correct mathematical notation (e.g., definite integrals, sine functions)
Multiple representations (graphs, tables, algebraic expressions) support the argument
Clear algebraic steps for parameter determination enhance reader comprehension
weaknesses: Frequent notation slips (e.g., âbcâ instead of âbxâ, inconsistent italicisation)
Inconsistent domain notation and missing units on integral results impair clarity
Axes on some screenshots are crowded and scaling is hard to read
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The communication demonstrates good logical structure and use of representations (band 3), but numerous notation inconsistencies, typos and missing units impede clarity, reducing this criterion to a âlimited use of appropriate mathematical language and representationâ level. A score of 2 reflects these persistent weaknesses despite solid underlying structure.

strengths: Personal motivation rooted in family health history shows clear engagement
Independent choice of self-collected ECG data and piecewise modelling demonstrates initiative
Use of Logger Pro and bespoke parameter selection indicates creative application
weaknesses: Techniques remain within standard IA methods without novel extension
Approach mirrors typical exploration structure, limiting originality
No advanced investigation (e.g., error analysis or optimisation) to elevate personal engagement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows clear personal engagement and some independent thinking (band 2), but lacks the originality and innovative extension required for the highest level. While the choice of data and modelling approach is personal, the methods are familiar, warranting a final score of 2.

strengths: Identifies strengths and limitations of the model and suggests realistic extensions (e.g., Fourier series, larger dataset)
Provides thoughtful, general evaluation of outcomes and clinical implications
Reflects meaningfully on methodological choices (e.g., intercept form, rounding decisions)
weaknesses: Reflection remains descriptive rather than deeply critical or quantitative
No error bounds or validation against additional data reduce analytical depth
Impact of key assumptions (e.g., perfect wave shape) is not rigorously assessed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is thoughtful and identifies outcomes, limitations and future directions (band 2), but it lacks the critical depth and quantitative analysis needed for higher marks. The evaluation is clear but not comprehensive, supporting a score of 2.

strengths: Effectively applies quadratic, sinusoidal and constant functions directly relevant to ECG modelling
Consistent SL-level techniques (algebraic manipulation, parameter solving, definite integration) used throughout
Correct assembly of a piecewise function covering the full cycle
Demonstrates thorough understanding with mostly correct calculations and appropriate precision
weaknesses: Minor algebraic slips (duplicate integral lines) and occasional missing units
Brief, unsupported mention of higher-level methods (Fourier series, BÃ©zier curves) without deeper justification
No overlay of the model on the actual trace limits visual validation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics used is fully commensurate with SL expectations and applied effectively across the exploration (band 4). Despite small inaccuracies and lack of direct model-trace comparison, the work shows good understanding and accuracy. A score of 4 reflects this strong, coherent mathematical application.

strandSelections.reasoning: Within each section the student sets up definitions, works through calculations and interprets results. Line-by-line algebra is shown for integrals, and explanations link computations to the medical aim. Occasional algebraic typos and repeated lines slightly detract from absolute clarity but the logical structure remains good.

Working shows correct parameter determination, domain identification and integration limits; numeric answers are plausible. Minor algebraic slips (duplication of an integral line, lack of units) are present but do not materially affect results. This indicates good understanding with only small inaccuracies, matching band 4.

Graphs from Logger Pro and Desmos, algebraic expressions, a table of contents and occasional diagrams of the heart give several complementary representations. These are pertinent to the argument and generally clear, though axes on some screenshots are crowded and scaling of the sinusoid in Fig 9 is hard to read, preventing the highest standard.

The report follows a clear narrative: rationale, data collection, modelling, domain justification, integration, and evaluation. Transitions are generally smooth and each mathematical decision builds logically on the previous one (e.g., intercept form â parameter a â domain â piece-wise assembly). Minor lapses such as duplicated headings and occasional digressions into background medicine do not break overall flow but prevent the exemplary standard.

Employing piecewise modelling, using Logger Pro examine points, and integrating areas for diagnostic thresholds shows some creativity and initiative, but these are common IA strategies. No particularly novel mathematical technique or significant extension (e.g., optimisation, error analysis) is attempted, keeping the strand at a âgoodâ level.

Strengths and limitations are identified (perfect-shape assumption, single-patient sample) and possible extensions (Fourier series, larger dataset) are suggested. While thoughtful, the analysis stays at a surface level and does not, for instance, quantify the impact of assumptions or compare alternative models, keeping it within âthoughtfulâ rather than âdeep and insightfulâ.

Outcomes (areas of waves, usefulness for automated diagnosis) are evaluated in general terms and future improvements are proposed. The student does not validate the model against additional data nor provide error bounds, so evaluation is clear but not comprehensive.

The student reflects on why intercept form was preferred, why rounding levels were chosen, and connects results to potential clinical applicationâthis is meaningful reflection. Nonetheless, comments are mostly descriptive rather than critically probing methodology or assumptions in depth, so the higher band is not met.

Variables, parameters and functions are defined, and standard notation for definite integrals, sine functions and intercept form is used correctly. Minor slips (e.g., ây = ax^2 + bc + câ, inconsistent italicisation) occur but do not impede understanding; overall usage is largely consistent.

The exploration is clearly motivated by the studentâs personal interest in cardiology and family health history, giving it a recognisable personal flavour. While the structure still mirrors standard IA layouts, the chosen data and medical framing provide clear evidence of a personal approach.

Most writing is relevant to modelling the ECG, yet the student devotes sizeable space to medical background, personal anecdotes and duplicate explanations of simple formulae. This lengthens the paper (19 pages) without advancing the mathematics, so conciseness is only âmostlyâ achieved.

Quadratic, sinusoidal and constant functions are directly relevant to ECG shapes, and definite integrals correctly address the stated diagnostic aims. The mathematics chosen is neither trivial nor excessive for SL, and it is applied coherently across the whole cycle, amounting to âeffectiveâ rather than merely âappropriateâ use.

Choosing a self-collected ECG trace, deciding to model each section with separate elementary functions, and justifying parameter choices show noticeable independent thinking. However, modelling techniques stay within textbook methods and do not demonstrate the depth or originality needed for the top band.

A contents page, numbered sections and sub-sections (Introduction, Aim, Body, Conclusion, Strengths, Limitations) demonstrate a deliberate structure. Figures are labelled and referenced inside the text, supporting easy navigation. Some repetition (two âConclusionâ entries) and uneven page numbering show that organisation is very good rather than exceptional.

All techniquesâalgebraic manipulation, function transformations, solving for parameters, definite integrationâsit squarely at SL level and are used throughout. The breadth and sustained application meet the descriptor âfully commensurateâ, though they do not rise to a level warranting an exceptional score (e.g., no higher-level calculus or statistical analysis).

annotations.comment: The works cited list draws on diverse sources, demonstrating thorough research to support the exploration.
The introduction provides extensive medical background on hypertension; streamline this to focus more directly on framing the mathematical modelling problem.
Axes and units of the ECG graph are clearly defined (time in seconds, voltage in mV), supporting precise later modelling.
Introduce the notation P(t) and T(t) when first describing the P and T waves to prepare the reader for their subsequent algebraic treatment.
Link the personal motivation directly to the choice of piecewise functions by explaining how this approach addresses the clinical needs you described.
The aim is stated clearly and justifies using piecewise linear, quadratic, and trigonometric functions at SL level.
Mentioning Fourier series and BÃ©zier curves is brief; explain why they exceed SL scope (e.g., course syllabus reference) for stronger justification.
The data collection method using Logger Pro and Vernier EKG sensor is appropriate, showing careful experimentation.
The table of contents is comprehensive and maps all major sections, aiding navigation through this extensive exploration.
There is a duplicate âConclusionâ entry in the contents, which creates redundancy and may confuse the reader; consider removing one instance.
The personal rationale rooted in family health history demonstrates clear personal engagement with the topic.
The narrative about hospital admission logistics, while interesting, is tangential to the mathematical focus; consider condensing.
The title is descriptive and clearly indicates the focus on ECG modelling in hypertension patients, providing an effective entry point for the reader.
The subtitle could be sharpened by mentioning the specific mathematical methods (e.g., piecewise functions) to signal the analytic approach earlier.
Rounding to five significant figures is appropriate given the small magnitudes, showing attention to precision.
Discussing the relative sizes of P and T wave areas in clinical terms would strengthen the evaluation of outcomes.
Equation (4) shows âbcâ instead of âbxâ; correct this typo to maintain standard notation.
Ensure consistent italicisation of variable symbols (e.g., a, b, c) across text and equations for professional presentation.
Deriving a by substituting a single ârandomâ point risks bias; consider using multiple points or justifying selection.
Algebraic steps solving for a are presented clearly, benefiting readers following the calculation.
The assumption of perfect wave shapes needs deeper discussion on its effect on model accuracy in real ECG data.
Include axis labels and units on the examination screenshots to enhance reproducibility of point selection.
Demonstrating standard, intercept, and vertex forms of a quadratic shows strong mathematical communication.
Reflect on how rounding a to three decimal places might affect subsequent area integrals to assess numerical impact.
The modeling of T wave replicates the P wave method, reinforcing methodological consistency.
Specify context for the random point K on T(t), such as why that time coordinate was chosen for solving a.
Notation shifts between Î±, bÌ in text and a, b in equations; standardize variable names to avoid confusion.
In solving for c, note the sine inverse branch choice and discuss how alternate solutions are excluded.
The piecewise function E(t) is assembled correctly, integrating all domain-specific sub-functions.
Ensure the domain endpoints in the piecewise definition use consistent â¤ or < notation to avoid overlaps or gaps.
The constant R(t) for PR/ST segments is correctly extracted from the inflection point, reflecting accurate baseline modelling.
Plotting the piecewise model on Desmos offers a strong visual check; include the actual ECG trace overlay for direct comparison.
The integration formula is stated clearly with proper notation, demonstrating accurate mathematical communication.
The area integral result lacks explicit units (e.g., mVÂ·s), which are crucial for physical interpretation.
The limitations section acknowledges sampling and functional assumptions but could quantify their impact (e.g., introduce error bounds).
Citation formats vary in URL presentation and missing access dates; apply a consistent referencing style throughout.
